results.label: Dysuria, flank pain, pyelonephritis, or AKI/hypovolemia
results.context:
General medicine
Adult
validated clinical intent workup
intent: Dysuria, flank pain, pyelonephritis, or AKI/hypovolemia
intent_id: gu_renal_dysuria_v1
intent tags: dysuria; UTI; pyelonephritis; flank_pain; AKI; hypovolemia; renal_colic; vitals; vital_signs; abdominal_pain; cva_tenderness; urinalysis; urine_culture; pregnancy; urosepsis; obstruction; renal_function; diagnostic_test; red_flag
clinical bundles: gu_renal
required domains: vitals; volume/perfusion; CVA/flank exam; abdominal add-on when indicated; urine and renal tests; urosepsis or obstruction red flags
avoid labels: PMI; Vibration sense; Visual acuity

safety.label: Blood pressure
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypotension, hypertension, shock physiology, pain response, medication effect, or disposition-changing instability.
safety.management_change: Abnormal blood pressure can change acuity, fluids, antihypertensive decisions, endocrine-crisis treatment, monitoring, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE

safety.label: Heart rate
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, shock physiology, adrenergic state, infection, pain, or medication effect.
safety.management_change: Abnormal heart rate can change ECG/telemetry urgency, volume or endocrine-crisis assessment, infection severity, medication review, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE

safety.label: Respiratory rate
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: respiratory distress, compensation, shock physiology, acid-base stress, sedation, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
safety.management_change: Abnormal respiratory rate or labored breathing can change oxygen/ventilation assessment, acid-base urgency, monitoring level, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE

safety.label: Oxygen saturation / support
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: hypoxemia, escalating oxygen need, respiratory failure risk, shock physiology, pulmonary disease, or cardiopulmonary deterioration.
safety.management_change: Abnormal oxygen saturation or rising oxygen requirement can change respiratory support, imaging urgency, pulmonary/infectious/cardiac framing, monitoring level, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE

safety.label: Temperature
safety.reason: Basic bedside safety data required for every validated clinical workup; not a physical exam maneuver.
safety.diagnostic_target: Basic bedside safety: fever, hypothermia, infection, drug reaction, endocrine crisis, inflammatory disease, or systemic illness.
safety.management_change: Fever or hypothermia can change infection/endocrine-crisis workup, isolation, medication decisions, monitoring, and escalation.
safety.evidence: AHRQ_CALIBRATE_DX; IDSA_CUTI_2025; EAU_URO_INFECTIONS; ACOG_UTI_PREGNANCY_2023; NICE_RENAL_STONES; JAMA_RCE

history.label: Are symptoms limited to dysuria/frequency/urgency, or are there fever/chills, flank pain, nausea/vomiting, hematuria, low urine output, stone history, pregnancy possibility, catheter/procedure, immunocompromise, or prior resistant urine culture?
history.reason: GU/renal source and severity: cystitis, pyelonephritis/systemic UTI, obstructed infected stone, renal colic, AKI/hypovolemia, catheter/procedure-associated infection, pregnancy, or resistant pathogen risk.
history.management_change: The answer changes whether to obtain urine culture, renal function/electrolytes, pregnancy testing, blood cultures/lactate, renal imaging, empiric antibiotic breadth, fluids, urology/OB escalation, or ED/inpatient monitoring.

core.label: Mouth exam
core.reason: Assesses volume status and perfusion before fluid or diuresis decisions.
core.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.

core.label: Bowel sounds
core.reason: Adds abdominal/GU source assessment when abdominal pain, vomiting, or urinary symptoms coexist.
core.diagnostic_target: murmur, abnormal heart sound, or S3/S4 clue
core.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.
core.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

core.label: Abdominal palpation
core.reason: Adds abdominal/GU source assessment when abdominal pain, vomiting, or urinary symptoms coexist.
core.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.
core.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

core.label: CVA tenderness
core.reason: Looks for pyelonephritis or renal colic when flank or urinary symptoms are present.
core.diagnostic_target: renal colic or pyelonephritis-associated renal tenderness
core.management_change: Renal tenderness or flank findings can support urine testing, imaging consideration, antimicrobial review, obstruction concern, or serial reassessment.
core.evidence: PubMed study of CVA tenderness in suspected ureteral stone; NCBI acute pyelonephritis review

core.label: JVP
core.reason: Assesses volume status and perfusion before fluid or diuresis decisions.
core.diagnostic_target: elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion
core.management_change: Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context.
core.evidence: Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension

core.label: Radial pulses
core.reason: Assesses volume status and perfusion before fluid or diuresis decisions.
core.diagnostic_target: perfusion deficit, vascular disease, or limb ischemia clue
core.management_change: Abnormal vitals or perfusion findings can trigger urgent reassessment, resuscitation, sepsis/shock pathway review, or escalation.
core.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

core.label: Lower extremity edema
core.reason: Assesses volume status and perfusion before fluid or diuresis decisions.
core.diagnostic_target: peripheral edema pattern and volume overload clue
core.management_change: Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context.
core.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

tests.label: Urine and renal-function tests
tests.reason: GU/renal symptoms need objective urine and renal-function data; exam alone cannot separate cystitis, pyelonephritis, stone, obstruction, AKI, pregnancy-related UTI, and urosepsis.
tests.diagnostic_target: Urine/renal diagnostic data: pyuria/bacteriuria/hematuria, culture/susceptibility, creatinine or electrolyte abnormality, pregnancy status, systemic infection, sepsis physiology, or medication-safety constraint.
tests.management_change: UA/culture, pregnancy status, creatinine/electrolytes, CBC, lactate, or blood-culture findings change antibiotic selection, renal dosing, fluid strategy, imaging, source-control/urology need, pregnancy pathway, and disposition.

tests.label: Renal obstruction and stone imaging pathway
tests.reason: Flank pain, hematuria, AKI, or systemic UTI can require imaging when obstruction, stone, abscess, hydronephrosis, or alternate diagnosis would change urgent management.
tests.diagnostic_target: Imaging target: hydronephrosis/obstruction, ureteral stone, infected obstructed system, renal abscess, urinary retention, or alternate abdominal/pelvic pathology.
tests.management_change: Obstruction, infected stone, abscess, solitary kidney risk, or worsening renal function changes urology consultation, decompression/source control, antibiotics, analgesia, fluids, admission, and imaging follow-up.

red_flags.label: Urosepsis and pyelonephritis escalation cues
red_flags.reason: A GU workup must distinguish uncomplicated cystitis from pyelonephritis, systemic UTI, and urosepsis because management and disposition change immediately.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: Systemic urinary infection clue: fever/rigors, flank/CVA symptoms, sepsis physiology, altered mentation, vomiting, pregnancy, immunocompromise, catheter/procedure association, resistance risk, or renal dysfunction.
red_flags.management_change: These cues change urine culture and blood culture/lactate threshold, empiric antibiotic route/breadth, fluids, renal dosing, imaging/source-control urgency, OB/urology involvement, and ED/inpatient escalation.

red_flags.label: Obstruction, stone, and AKI danger cues
red_flags.reason: Stone/obstruction and AKI red flags should not be hidden inside CVA tenderness or volume-status exams.
red_flags.diagnostic_target: High-risk renal process: obstructed infected system, urinary retention, severe renal colic, solitary/transplant kidney risk, gross hematuria, worsening AKI, or dangerous electrolyte abnormality.
red_flags.management_change: These cues change imaging urgency, urology/nephrology consultation, decompression/source control, analgesia/antiemetic route, fluid strategy, electrolyte correction, and ED/inpatient disposition.

top_retrieved.label: Blood pressure
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal vitals or perfusion findings can trigger urgent reassessment, resuscitation, sepsis/shock pathway review, or escalation.
top_retrieved.evidence: Stanford Medicine 25 blood pressure measurement techniques; Surviving Sepsis Campaign guideline

top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal vitals or perfusion findings can trigger urgent reassessment, resuscitation, sepsis/shock pathway review, or escalation.

top_retrieved.label: Mouth exam
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.

top_retrieved.label: Oropharynx
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.

top_retrieved.label: Bowel sounds
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: murmur, abnormal heart sound, or S3/S4 clue
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.
top_retrieved.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

top_retrieved.label: Abdominal palpation
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.
top_retrieved.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

top_retrieved.label: CVA tenderness
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: renal colic or pyelonephritis-associated renal tenderness
top_retrieved.management_change: Renal tenderness or flank findings can support urine testing, imaging consideration, antimicrobial review, obstruction concern, or serial reassessment.
top_retrieved.evidence: PubMed study of CVA tenderness in suspected ureteral stone; NCBI acute pyelonephritis review

top_retrieved.label: JVP
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: elevated right-sided filling pressure or venous congestion
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal congestion findings can support diuresis decisions, escalation of respiratory support assessment, daily volume trending, or need for cardiac evaluation in context.
top_retrieved.evidence: Stanford Medicine 25 neck vein exam; physical examination systematic review for pulmonary hypertension

top_retrieved.label: Abdominal percussion
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: tenderness pattern, bowel activity, organomegaly, or ascites clue
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.
top_retrieved.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: tenderness pattern, bowel activity, organomegaly, or ascites clue
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal or changed findings improve focused bedside documentation and can prompt targeted reassessment, diagnostics, or escalation in context.

top_retrieved.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

top_retrieved.label: Murphy sign
top_retrieved.management_change: Positive acute-abdomen signs can prompt senior review, imaging, surgical consultation, NPO/analgesia planning, or serial abdominal exams.
top_retrieved.evidence: Open review of abdominal eponyms; WSES acute calculous cholecystitis guideline

top_retrieved.label: Rebound tenderness
top_retrieved.management_change: Positive acute-abdomen signs can prompt senior review, imaging, surgical consultation, NPO/analgesia planning, or serial abdominal exams.
top_retrieved.evidence: Open acute abdominal pain review and guideline-style public references

top_retrieved.label: Psoas sign
top_retrieved.management_change: Positive acute-abdomen signs can prompt senior review, imaging, surgical consultation, NPO/analgesia planning, or serial abdominal exams.
top_retrieved.evidence: AAFP appendicitis review citing clinical likelihood ratios; JAMA Rational Clinical Examination PubMed record

top_retrieved.label: Radial pulses
top_retrieved.diagnostic_target: perfusion deficit, vascular disease, or limb ischemia clue
top_retrieved.management_change: Abnormal vitals or perfusion findings can trigger urgent reassessment, resuscitation, sepsis/shock pathway review, or escalation.
top_retrieved.evidence: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA heart failure guideline and public vascular/edema review sources

top_retrieved.label: Obturator sign
top_retrieved.management_change: Positive acute-abdomen signs can prompt senior review, imaging, surgical consultation, NPO/analgesia planning, or serial abdominal exams.
top_retrieved.evidence: JAMA Rational Clinical Examination appendicitis PubMed record; open acute-abdomen review

review_notes.detail: Blood pressure (198): Replaced by the validated blood pressure safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.
Heart rate (186): Replaced by the validated heart rate safety-floor item so basic bedside data stays separate from physical exam maneuvers.
Oropharynx (181): Not promoted because the selected validated clinical intent did not define this as a core or conditional bedside item.
Abdominal percussion (137): Not promoted because the selected validated clinical intent did not define this as a core or conditional bedside item.
Spleen palpation (136): Spleen palpation needs splenomegaly, hematologic, malignancy, or systemic lymphadenopathy context.
Abdominal inspection (133): Not promoted because the selected validated clinical intent did not define this as a core or conditional bedside item.
Murphy sign (126): Murphy sign needs RUQ, biliary, gallbladder, or jaundice context.
Rebound tenderness (126): Advanced abdominal maneuvers need localized abdominal, hepatobiliary, or peritoneal concern.
